channel.title: Frontion News - Global Geopolitics Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: 🏛️ Senate Advances Iran War Powers Resolution as Republican Cassidy Flips
item.description: The US Senate voted 50-47 to advance legislation forcing Trump to withdraw from the Iran war, with Louisiana Senator Bill Cassidy joining Democrats after his primary loss. The vote signals growing Republican unease with the prolonged conflict.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇷🇺 Xi and Putin Meet in Beijing Days After Trump Visit, Sign 40 Cooperation Agreements
item.description: Chinese and Russian leaders hailed their strategic ties and growing energy trade, signing over 40 agreements in technology, trade, and media. Xi called ties "the highest level in history" but no progress was made on the Power of Siberia 2 gas pipeline Russia had pushed for.

item.title: 🇮🇷⚔️ Iran Displays Weapons at Public Events as Tehran Readies for Potential War Resumption
item.description: Iranian Revolutionary Guard members are conducting public weapons training in Tehran, with ballistic missiles displayed at mass weddings. The demonstrations aim to project strength and motivate hardliners amid economic hardship and war uncertainty.

item.title: 🚢 Iran Claims IRGC Coordinated Transit of 26 Vessels Through Hormuz in 24 Hours
item.description: Iran says its Revolutionary Guard is controlling traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, publishing a new map marking a controlled maritime zone. The FAO warned the blockade could trigger a severe global food price crisis within 6-12 months.

item.title: 🇦🇹🕵️ Austrian Ex-Intelligence Officer Found Guilty of Spying for Russia
item.description: Former Austrian intelligence official Egisto Ott was sentenced to over four years in prison for passing information to Russian intelligence and Wirecard fugitive Jan Marsalek. The case has revived concerns about Austria as a hub for Russian espionage.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Developed Asia draws private capital as investors pivot away from China
item.description: Japan is leading a shift in private capital allocation as LPs prioritize exits, stronger governance, and proven returns, with Australia and Korea also gaining attention from investors seeking alternatives to China exposure.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia has finally turned its back on Europe, Berliner Zeitung reports
item.description: German newspaper Berliner Zeitung writes that Russia has reoriented itself to China, with EU-Russia trade shrinking from nearly $400 billion in 2013 to just $64 billion by 2025, an 80% decline since 2021.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Erdogan tells Trump Iran issues can be resolved as Turkey mediates
item.description: Turkish President Erdogan told Trump he welcomed the ceasefire extension with Iran and believes contested issues can be resolved. Turkey has been passing messages between Washington, Tehran, and mediators Pakistan.

item.title: 🇨🇺 US charges Cuba's Raúl Castro with murder over 1996 downing of planes
item.description: The US indicted former Cuban leader Raúl Castro on conspiracy charges over the 1996 shootdown of two civilian planes that killed four people. The move marks a sharp escalation in US-Cuba tensions.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Putin enjoys Xi's Chinese welcome but heads home without pipeline deal
item.description: Russia and China showed they were shoulder-to-shoulder on the world stage, but limits emerged as no progress was made on the Power of Siberia 2 gas pipeline. The "spirit of Beijing" contrasted with the fading "spirit of Anchorage" from Putin-Trump talks.

item.title: ⚔️ Russia 'dangerously' intercepts British spy plane over Black Sea
item.description: A British Rivet Joint reconnaissance aircraft was dangerously intercepted by a Russian fighter while securing NATO's eastern flank in international airspace. The UK Ministry of Defence called the incident "unsafe and unprofessional."

item.title: 💰 Iran's stock market reopens after lengthy war-related shutdown
item.description: Iran's stock exchange resumed trading after an extended closure due to the Iran war, with a controlled reopening that excluded stocks of companies hit by US and Israeli strikes such as energy and steel firms.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump may have hit Iran harder than publicly known
item.description: Reuters reports on questions around the full scope of US military action against Iran, with some analysts suggesting the strikes may have been more extensive than officially disclosed.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK cancels planned fuel tax rise as economic pressure mounts
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced the cancellation of a planned motor fuel tax increase as the government faces pressure over rising living costs linked to energy market disruptions.

item.title: 🦠 Ebola patient stable in German hospital; CDC confirms US missionary infected
item.description: A US missionary who contracted Ebola is in stable condition in a German hospital, while the CDC confirms monitoring of contacts. The outbreak in Congo continues to spread with over 600 cases and 139 suspected deaths.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iranian public receives basic gun training amid Trump threats of war resumption
item.description: Iran is conducting weapons demonstrations and public firearms training in Tehran, with hardliners encouraging citizens to prepare for potential conflict as the US threatens to resume military action.

item.title: 🇬🇱 US needs to put its footprint back on Greenland, US envoy to AFP
item.description: The US envoy to Greenland stated America needs to reestablish its presence on the island amid growing geopolitical competition in the Arctic region.

item.title: ⛪ South Lebanon cultural heritage going up in smoke amid conflict
item.description: France 24 reports on the destruction of cultural heritage sites in southern Lebanon as the Israel-Iran conflict continues to impact the broader region.

item.title: 🏝️ Iran consolidates Hormuz control with island checkpoints and diplomatic deals
item.description: Reuters investigation reveals Iran is strengthening its grip on the Strait of Hormuz through island checkpoints and diplomatic agreements with regional actors.

item.title: 🦠 Ebola outbreak in Congo sparks US response concerns
item.description: The New York Times reports on the Ebola outbreak in Congo and questions about the US government's response as cases exceed 600 with 139 suspected deaths.

item.title: ✈️ Vilnius airport suspends flights due to drone warning
item.description: Lithuania's Vilnius airport temporarily suspended flights following a drone warning, highlighting ongoing security concerns in the Baltic region near Russia and Belarus.

item.title: 🏦 Bank of England has time to gauge Iran war impact, Bailey says
item.description: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey indicated the central bank has time to assess the economic impact of the Iran war on inflation and financial markets before making policy decisions.

item.title: 🛢️ Saudi Arabia crude exports drop to record low in March
item.description: Saudi Arabia's crude oil exports fell to a record low in March according to JODI data, as the kingdom adjusts production amid global energy market disruptions from the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US military jets tracked near Cuba as tensions rise
item.description: Tracking data shows several US Navy reconnaissance flights near Cuba as tensions escalate following the indictment of former Cuban leader Raúl Castro over the 1996 plane shootdown.
